text: Databases are essential for storing and organizing information. There are two main types: SQL (structured) databases, which use tables with rows and columns, and NoSQL databases, which handle unstructured data like documents or graphs. In SQL databases, you write queries to retrieve specific data, such as "SELECT * FROM customers". Data modeling involves designing tables and relationships to avoid redundancy. Indexes speed up queries by creating a map of the data, making searches faster. Understanding these concepts helps you choose the right database for your needs.
